All right, welcome back to our template on Market Search for Meaning. We're now going to take a quick look at a few examples so you can see how this works in action. So just so you know, this example is in your workbook which you should have access to somewhere below this video. Cool. So first one is taken from the holistic health space. A little bit of background. This is a holistic psychiatrist who is offering a protocol of diet, meditation, and other exercises and protocols to help women taper off of antidepressants. So these are women who are ready to taper off of antidepressants. They don't want to be on them any longer. And the research here indicates that they feel a little bit of shame and guilt of having gotten on them to begin with. So that is what we are going to use as the basis of our self-forgiveness. Cool. So subject, if you could go back in time, would you change this moment? And here we go. We jump right into the story of how that came to be. So there was a moment, a seemingly innocent one, one month, one year, or even 10 years ago, where you sat in an office, hands clasped together, posture bent forward, a moment of suspended anticipation where you're well-intentioned yet misinformed doctor pulled out a prescription pad. You watched eagerly as he mindlessly scribbled a name on a piece of paper. Prozac, Paxil, Zoloft, a moment that lasted all of three seconds. The sound of your doctor ripping the page from the pad probably still echoes deep in your memory. So notice how we're using a lot of sensual imagery, engaging sound, engaging sight, really painting the scene here to put our reader back in that chair. The reassuring smile that coaxed you into taking it from his hand and the instant sense of relief you experienced while pocketing it. A promise of hope, the promise that you could beat your depression with a cocktail of chemicals more foreign to you and your body than anything you could ever fathom that to time. Perhaps in the back of your mind, there was a tiny voice saying, do I really want to depend on this pill for the rest of my life? Yet the quick fix relief and trust in the medical system was more than enough to sweep aside any sense of doubt. And it was this overwhelming sense of relief and the system that reinforced it that blinded you to the sheer fact that you had just pocketed a life sentence. Fortunately, it's also a reversible one. And it's important to remind yourself that this critical moment was not your fault. So here we are taking this sequence, this event in their lives that they could be perceiving as one that is shameful or one that they were implicit in creating and offering that it wasn't their fault. And now I'll explain why. For myself and the hundreds of women I've treated, we would have given anything to travel back to this moment and take a more informed choice, a more courageous one. It were also self-forgiving to the fact that we made the choice we truly believed was best for us at the time. In many cases, the only choice available to us. And in doing so, let go of any guilt, shame and yes, even anger in admitting that we may have put our minds and bodies through with that decision. I can't offer you a redo, but I can offer you a recovery. So now we're offering them a better way. Drug free, side effect free. The number leaving this blank for now, the X system is the exact system of self healing program that's been behind hundreds of recoveries of even the most skeptical women who believe they would have never be able to get off the meds. And for the 245 women who already said yes to the better group of full return to mind and body health is just a few short weeks away. If you'd like to join him, the same reset is here waiting for you. All you have to do is lean in with the same anticipation you did when you took that first prescription paper. So at this time with the reassurance that you'll experience that what you'll experience isn't an artificial quick fix, but a lifelong relationship with your more vibrant, radiant, joyful self. So here we're taking the quality they exhibited in the first case, you know, having that kind of anticipation and that willingness to do it and using that for, you know, for our offer, our protocol. So if you're ready for that, let's revoke your life sentence and reclaim your wholeness and love and healing and the sign off. So here you could see the moment clearly stated that the beginning, this is what brings them shame and guilt and all these things that could prevent them from taking a new, more empowering decision. Now we deflect the blame to something more external. It was really all they could do. You know, the moment happened so fast, the doctor just gave them that pad and they didn't really have any way to say no or look into other alternatives. We bridge into a very clear reason why it wasn't their fault, but how it was actually a good thing. You know, the most skillful thing they had at the time and probably save them from, you know, further pain and suffering in some way, but then we offer them a more aligned, more appropriate way through our protocol now because these are people who want to get off their antidepressants and not be tied to it forever. So we offer them to take the same eagerness they had when they first got on the medication and now use that same eagerness to go on a protocol that's offered in the product. So yeah, that's really how it plays out in this first example. And now we'll take a look at a second example, which is more in the software as a service world. This was for Derek Halpern's Zippy courses, I believe, and this was an affiliate email promoting it. Cool. So a few subject line variations via this business coach. If your business coach ever says this dot, dot, dot, entrepreneurial shaming at its worst, gur, stop procrastinating, gur. Cool. So here we go. Stop procrastinating. It's like the low hanging fruit that a rookie business coach will call you out on whenever your big dreams, i.e. creating a profitable online course, get put on hold. And personally, I can't stand it. It's condescending, presumptuous, downright degrading, and worst of all, a million miles removed from the truth. Because the thing is procrastination isn't a cause. It's an effect of not having the confidence to go all in on your prospect, often a legitimate protective one that saves you and your family from needless turmoil and overwhelm, endless nights trying to find the one tech guy who actually gets what you're trying to build and won't charge your half your annual income to do it. And a quickly dissolving bank account without the eventual payoff or end product to show for it, which makes for awkward convoes with the fam. So here we're not taking as specific of a story as the previous example, but this is really all about how a would be online course creator feels shamed by their business coaches or just their peers for having procrastinated in creating it. So that's the more shameful moment or the misstep, the fact that they're procrastinating and not getting it out the door, not getting it done. And here is really the reasons it's not their fault or the pain that it spared them from. So this is why it was actually skillful that they didn't just, you know, screw it, let's do it kind of mentality, but actually waited. So in this case, we're showing how procrastination wasn't a bad thing, but probably something that saved them from even further struggle. So I'm here to clear the air, particularly when it comes to building your time leveraged and profitable online course. It's one thing to have an Evernote or Google Docs stack with ideas content lessons modules, but a whole other thing to actually feel confident about putting it out in the world. And unlike what the finger pointing business coach might tell you, you haven't procrastinated in getting your amazing course out of your head and into the world. You haven't chosen to play small. So just continuing to play up that theme. And you definitely don't have any limiting beliefs around the massive impact and exciting new revenue streams available to you. You simply and appropriately waited at waited it out for a better time. So this is where our reader who has maybe procrastinated in the past or not gotten things done like they really hope to. This is where they feel maybe a little more validated for having done it where they could really think, Oh, you know what, maybe I haven't been slacking off for the last two years. And it's a good thing that I waited until now. I was actually smart in doing that. For a better time, one way you can build it with confidence, knowing that you'll be spared from the same tech horse stories and customer repelling blunders that have plagued dozens of your entrepreneurial peers. Because you've heard the horror stories. And even though you've repeatedly told yourself that won't happen to me, a more rational part of you knows that unless you take an actual step in protecting yourself from the same tech failures, it'll be destined to be just another angry voice and tech haters anonymous with a half finished online course and a fully emptied bank account. So that is what has kept you procrastinating on your online course. So we're really playing up how procrastinating has been a good thing. So this is all really reflecting back the one sentence that a lot of marketers use, but don't unpack, which is it's not your fault. So not only is it not your fault, but you actually made a really wise decision in procrastinating. What has kept you from serving your market with your well owned experience, expertise and unique way of doing things and from having a time lever just to freeze you from the time for money trap while growing your revenue to exciting new levels. And while Zippy courses does so much amazing stuff to remove these barriers, what it really does better than anything we've ever seen is it gives you permission to hit play and to fire the coach who ever had the nerve to call you lazy forever in your corner. Amy. Cool. And then we go into some features and benefits back to tie back into ways that it ends procrastination. We won't, we won't read all of these here, but they are here if you want to take a look later. So two very different contexts, one of them in a more serious niche for people who have really struggled with some serious issues. And we're confronting that compassionately yet directly. And the other one is in more of a marketing or SaaS context, more of a generalized story. So taking something that everybody has heard, we don't need to tell a specific story of when they were told to stop procrastinating. But the thing they have both in common is we're taking something that the market feels a negative self perception around some shame, some guilt around and showing how it was either the most skillful thing they could have done or something that actually was a good thing and save them from further issues. And now we could take those same good qualities and then empower them to take a new decision that actually helps them. Cool. So those are two examples for market search for meaning.
